articles.title: Air Plant
articles.content: A sponge-like surface plant that produces and stores air.
articles.useCommon: A fist sized piece can be held in the mouth to provide a medium creature with 5 minutes of air before the plant wilts and the recipient is back to normal breathing conditions for whatever the environment determines. This effect works underwater, in clouds of poison or dangerous spores, sandstorms, and other similar conditions.
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Air[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Alcohol
articles.content: A liquid formed but the fermentation of various plants. Two common forms are wine, made from various fruits (primarily grapes) and beer, made from different grains.
articles.useCommon:
The most common use is as an intoxicant to aid in having a good time or drowning one's sorrows. 
Also used by @[herbalist's](profession:1848d3a7-6f83-44af-a930-7fd4c9e351d0) as a base for making [i]common[/i] potions.
articles.properties: Highly flammable
articles.origin: Created by @[vintners](profession:08a6c422-4b8f-4f5d-8ea4-65f75a0bf5b1), @[brewers](profession:508e198b-a126-4c3e-9ce5-899bd2b234f0), and @[alchemists](profession:758e627f-aaca-4874-80a5-7a28af7a97dd), depending on the intended use.
articles.manufacturing: Can be distilled to further increase the concentration, producing various products such as whisky, gin, vodka etc.
articles.value: Varies depending on specific formulation and intended use.

articles.title: Alkahest
articles.content: Alkahest is the universal solvent, capable  of dissolving any composite substance, including gold, without altering or destroying its fundamental components. By extracting from composite substances their fundamental virtues and properties, alchemists hoped to gain control of invaluable medical healing properties.
articles.useCommon:
Used by @[alchemists](profession:758e627f-aaca-4874-80a5-7a28af7a97dd) to break down verious compounds into their constituent components.

Also used by @[herbalists](profession:1848d3a7-6f83-44af-a930-7fd4c9e351d0) as a base for making [i]legendary[/i] potions.
articles.properties: Highly corrosive
articles.origin: @[Alchemists](profession:758e627f-aaca-4874-80a5-7a28af7a97dd) who create alkhest guard their reagents and process very carefully. It is passed down from master to specially deserving students.

articles.title: Aluminum Grease
articles.content: A silvery paste prepared by @[alchemists](profession:758e627f-aaca-4874-80a5-7a28af7a97dd), used to lubricate machinery and as a base in making [i]very rare [/i] magical potions.
articles.useCommon: Used by @[herbalists](profession:1848d3a7-6f83-44af-a930-7fd4c9e351d0) as a base for making [i]very rare[/i] potions.

articles.title: Aqua Regia
articles.content: A highly corrosive liquid known  to dissolve gold and platnum along with biologicals.
articles.useCommon: Used by @[herbalists](profession:1848d3a7-6f83-44af-a930-7fd4c9e351d0) as a base for making [i]uncommon[/i] potions.
articles.properties: Highly corrosive
articles.colour: Freshly prepared aqua regia is colorless, but it turns yellow, orange, or red within seconds

articles.title: Arctic Creeper
articles.content: A low, gray-stemmed creeping plant that grows around rock formations in high, cold climates.
articles.useCommon: The leaves can be dried and ground, and added as an ingredient to many common poisons to change the damage type to cold or necrotic.
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Water[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Arrowroot
articles.content: An unusual, wood-stemmed plant dappled brown and white. The roots are very shallow in soil and extend vertically to form a teepee like structure at the base of the plant.
articles.useCommon: The stem can be dried and ground and mixed with oil to create a frothy polishing oil that can add a +1 to a weapon’s damage without the need for magic or craftsmanship for a 8 hours.
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Air[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Basilisk Breath
articles.content: A smallish, evergreen bush resembling the dwarf holly, but produces pale gray berries instead of the typical bright red berries of the holly.
articles.useCommon: These berries can be juiced to create a strong paralytic poison that can be applied to a slashing or piercing weapon. A target damaged by the weapon must make a Wisdom saving throw or is slowed as if by the slow spell. Additionally, the creature must make a Constitution saving throw at the end of each of it's turns for 4 rounds. On a failed save, the target is paralyzed for 1 minute. The DC for both the Wisdom and Constitution saving throws is equal to 8 + the Nature skill of the person who prepared the poison. The potency of this poison dissipates after a successful strike.
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Shadow[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Bloodgrass
articles.content: A wide-bladed grass with a red-tinted central vein running the length of the blade that grows in small bunches.
articles.useCommon: Bloodgrass can be ground into a thick brown paste that can be added and mixed into most potions and drinks to become adequate food equivalent to a day’s rations for the party.
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Life[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Bloodstaunch
articles.content: A small leafy herb that is often found in gullies and ravines in arid regions and foothills. The herb has bright green leaves, and smells faintly like honey crossed with fresh rainfall
articles.useCommon: From you leaves a resin can be extracted that can be applied to open wounds to quickly staunch bleeding. Applying the herb to a bleeding wound grants the recipient recovery of 1 hit point and confers immunity to bleed damage for 1 hour.
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Earth[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Blueleaf
articles.content: Known for the gleaming blue color of it's leaves, the tree often grows in thick copses throughout the north. The thin trunks of the trees are remarkably durable, despite high winds and heavy snowfall and one use of the tree is the wood, which is often used by the Volodni craftsmen to create weapons and armor.
articles.useCommon: Magically treating the wood, called “bluewood”, causes it to become as hard as steel though only weighing half as much (crafting with bluewood requires special training from a Volodni craftsman). [p]High-priced entertainers often bum small pieces of the wood, as it creates leaping blue flames. [p]The leaves and sap are often ground in the creation of a vivid blue dye that is used to dye clothes in the north, and sometimes in the creation of tattoo ink.
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Air[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Calcium Grease
articles.content: Tallow-based greases were the earliest lubricants developed by @[alchemists](profession:758e627f-aaca-4874-80a5-7a28af7a97dd) and as a result are  relatively inexpensive to manufacture and are still in use.
articles.useCommon: Used by @[herbalists](profession:1848d3a7-6f83-44af-a930-7fd4c9e351d0) as a base for making [i]common[/i] potions.

articles.title: Cat's Tail
articles.content: Cat's Tail is a small plant with long, thin, spear-shaped leaves rising from its base, and a single long and thin flower atop the stalk.
articles.useCommon: When ground into a salve, it will remedy all pain in a person's feet over the course of a short rest.
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Water[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Chonosmith Brass
articles.content: A special alloy named in honor of @[Constantine Chronosmith](person:4397abe5-31b6-4eab-b75f-ac79fd772e17), this brass is particularly resistant to wear and corrosion. It's favored in the creation of long-lasting gears and clockwork mechanisms.A special alloy named in honor of @[Constantine Chronosmith](person:4397abe5-31b6-4eab-b75f-ac79fd772e17), this brass is particularly resistant to wear and corrosion. It's favored in the creation of long-lasting gears and clockwork mechanisms.

articles.title: Chromus Slime
articles.content: A yellow-orange slime mold found growing in dark, humid places, near a supply of constant water. The slime mold is capable of filtering out the salt in salt water and can therefore live in fresh water or by the ocean.
articles.useCommon: When combined with high­grade alcohol and added to a potion or poison chromus slime reverses it's effect, creating the opposite effects to what the potion or poison would typically do.
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Fire[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Corpsebomb
articles.content:
The fungus begins life as a tiny spore that floats along until inhaled by a creature, and lodges itself in the moist tissue of the lungs. The spore then begins to grow, causing irritation, pain, and finally death once the fungus has reached a sizable mass. Once the host creature has perished, the fungus increases growth rapidly until it pushes a hollow tendril forth from the flesh. 
At the exposed end of the tendril the fungus begins growing a thin membrane, which is inflated by the gases produced by the rotting body.
articles.useCommon:
When the membrane ruptures and disperses the spores, each living creature in a 40-foot radius sphere must make a Constitution saving throw (DC 10). 
- On a failed save a target inhales spores, and each hour thereafter must repeat the Constitution saving throw with a cumulative -1 penalty to the roll Each hour the creature suffers a cumulative 1d6 piercing damage (2d6 on the second failure, 3d6 on the third, etc.) as the fungus spreads through it's internal organs. 
- A single success stops the process and renders the fungus inert. [p]If the fungus catches fire the spore sack soon explodes, and each creature in a 20-foot radius sphere must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC 15).  A target takes 3d6 fire damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful save. All spores are incinerated in this process.
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Fire[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Creeping Mangrove
articles.content:
Growing along the edge of the swamps, it's gnarled roots reach down into the brackish water, and spindly branches protruding vertically towards the sky. The branches are covered with large amounts of small oval-shaped leaves. During warmer seasons the trees bud small hard fruits that drop off to float along the slight currents of a swamp. 

This species of mangrove has flourished in areas where others have perished, due to their ability to slowly pull up roots and migrate to areas of higher moisture or more favorable alkalinity. The mangroves have been known to uproot small trees and brush, or simply walk over them, trampling them down.
articles.useCommon: Wood from these trees burns readily, even when sopping wet.
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Water[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Darkberry
articles.content: Short bushes with spindly branches and fluffy green leaves, the plant is known for the unusual berries that only a few produce. The small purple berries contain essence from the Plane of Shadow, and the bushes are said to only grow where the Shadow Weave is strongest.
articles.useCommon: When a darkberry is split or crushed, it releases shadowstuff, creating a circle of magical darkness with a 5-foot radius sphere, lasting for 1 minute.
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Shadow[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Devilweed Wyssin
articles.content: A dull-coloreed grass, with sharp awns,
articles.useCommon: Can be rolled and smoked like tobacco. Doing so provides +2 to Strength-based ability checks and -1 to all other ability checks for 1d6 hours
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Fire[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Distilled Alcohol
articles.content:
By taking a basic form of alcohol and distilling it down, a higher concentration is achieved and increasing the intoxication factor. Infusing the result with various essences - during or after the distillation process - imparts  distinct flavours.

Further distillation - double or triple distillation - of the resulting liquid is possible, further raising the concentraion of alcohol, and intoxication effect.
articles.useCommon: Consumed as an aid to a good time or to drown one's sorrow. Also used by @[herbalists](profession:1848d3a7-6f83-44af-a930-7fd4c9e351d0) as a base for making [i]rare[/i] magical potions.
articles.properties: Highly flammable
articles.origin: Created by @[distillers](profession:fcfec767-1482-4ca4-ae66-fb8920424ce5) and @[alchemists](profession:758e627f-aaca-4874-80a5-7a28af7a97dd),  depending on the intended use.

articles.title: Dragontears
articles.content: This rare, small, translucent succulent grows primarily in the low desert.
articles.useCommon: When crushed, it exudes a sweet, milky white substance with numbing properties. [p]Properly prepared, determined by succeeding on an Intelligence (Nature) check (DC 15), Dragontear unguent promotes rapid healing, healing for 3d8 hit points and preventing scarring on successful application. [p]The unguent can be fatal when prepared incorrectly and applied to an open wound, as it's numbing properties can cause choking or heart failure.
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Water[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Dragonwort
articles.content: This plant has a gnarled, erect and cyclindrical stem. It has wavy leavees, dark green on top and blusih underneath. It has pink flowers that cluseter in a spike at the top of the stem.
articles.useCommon: The root of this herb should be boiled over a low heat for three days, and the infusion drunk by people with the plauge, in order to cure some symptoms or the plague as a whole (60% chnace to cure completely). Because of this, the value of this herb increases greatly in times of plague.
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Life[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Earthheart Emerald
articles.content:
These deep green crystals are vibrant and richly colored, pulsing with a soft, inner light. Ranging in size from that of a pebble to as large as a melon, each Earthheart Emerald is a marvel of nature's craftsmanship. They are raw and rugged, yet hold an untamed beauty that speaks of the elemental power locked within.

The value of an Earthheart Emerald greatly depends on its size, quality, and the amount of energy it holds. Prices could vary depending on the setting, availability of the emeralds, and demand for such a power source.

[container:tablebox-100]
[table]
[tr][th:12]Size[/th][th]Energy Content[/th][th:15]Value[/th][/tr]
[tr][td]Small[br](< 1")[/td][td][left]Power a simple device, such as a small clockwork item, for up to a month.[/left][/td][td]50 gp -[br] 200 gp[/td][/tr]
[tr][td]Medium[br](1"-4")[/td][td][left]Power a large mechanical object, like a mechanical golem, for up to a week.[/left][/td][td]500 gp -[br] 2,000 gp[/td][/tr]
[tr][td]Large[br](4"-8")[/td][td][left]Power an entire building's worth of machinery for a day.[/left][/td][td]5,000 gp - [br]20,000 gp[/td][/tr]
[/table]
[/container]
articles.useCommon:
Used long ago by artificers and for powering machines. To extract the power, a process of runic engraving (also lost to time) is required where the emerald is inscribed with specific runes.
[br][br]
articles.properties:
[b]Source of Power:[/b] The energy within an Earthheart Emerald is drawn from the elemental heart of the world itself. Over centuries, the earth's innate elemental power is absorbed and condensed within these crystals, resulting in a stone capable of holding vast amounts of energy. This energy is similar to geothermal power on a highly concentrated scale. 
[br][br]
articles.halflife:
An Earthheart Emerald can hold its charge indefinitely until the energy is extracted. Once the energy has been drained, the emerald becomes a regular stone. 
[br][br]
articles.commonState: Crystals of varing size - pebbles to small melons
articles.colour: Rich, deep green, pulsing with an inner light
articles.hazards:
If an emerald is cracked or damaged, the energy within might leak out prematurely or explode in a burst of power. The size of the explosion and its effects depend on how much energy was stored within the emerald.
[br][br]
articles.value: Varies with size, quality, energy content
articles.rarity: Legendary - no source exists in modern times
articles.storage:
Must be kept carefully packed to avoid physical damage potentially leading to an explosion.
[br][br]

articles.title: Ecru
articles.content: Small, red-leaved drooping palm trees that grow no more than 5-feet talk The trees produce a small, red, crystalline growths, as a result of build-up of the plants internal resins, called 'ecru crystal'.
articles.useCommon: This crystal can be dissolved in a strong acid and consumed to induce a state of blissful relaxation for 10 minutes. During this time, there is a 25% chance that a spellcaster consuming ecru crystal will receive a boost of 1d4-1 points to either Wisdom, Charisma, or Intelligence for 24 hours. During this period, the consumer’s Dexterity score is halved.
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Earth[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Elemental Iron
articles.content: A rare type of iron infused with elemental energy during the smelting process. This material is highly sought after for the creation of magical items and constructs.

articles.title: Ephedra
articles.content: This small succulent sedum produces small offshoots which hold between 8 and 16 small leaves.
articles.useCommon: These leaves can then be dried and ground into a powder to mix with a poultice or potion to increase the tier of a healing die by 1, turning a d6 into a d8, etc. This effect can be used during short rests or with healing magic as a poultice, or can be used by adding to a health potion
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Life[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Essence of Air
articles.content: One of the six mystical essences, it is a key ingredient used by @[herbalists](profession:1848d3a7-6f83-44af-a930-7fd4c9e351d0) / @[alchemists](profession:758e627f-aaca-4874-80a5-7a28af7a97dd)  alike.
articles.origin:
Created by @[herbalists](profession:1848d3a7-6f83-44af-a930-7fd4c9e351d0) and @[alchemists](profession:758e627f-aaca-4874-80a5-7a28af7a97dd) by processing various plants and other materials.

[tr]
   [th]Plants[/th][th]Other[/th]
[/tr]
[tr]
   [td][tagged:essence-air-plant|list|none][/td]
   [td]===[/td]
[/tr]
[/table]
articles.storage: Used immediately as it cannot be stored

articles.title: Essence of Earth
articles.content: One of the six mystical essences, it is a key ingredient used by @[herbalists](profession:1848d3a7-6f83-44af-a930-7fd4c9e351d0) and @[alchemists](profession:758e627f-aaca-4874-80a5-7a28af7a97dd) alike.

articles.storage: Used immediately as it cannot be stored

articles.title: Fairy Bells
articles.content: A leafy green plant that springs up in clusters, the majority of the plant is rather uninteresting, save for the violet flowers that seem to bloom almost year-round. When the wind blows through the flowers, their seeds rattle around inside and create a curious ringing sound, much like tiny bells.
articles.useCommon: Many rural children enjoy picking them and weaving the flowers into their garments, which then jingle as they run and twirl.[p]There is also a legend that the sound of the flowers ward off evil spirits, a legend that is partially true. Any fiend must make a DC 15 Wisdom save vs. fear in order to approach within 10 feet of the fairy bells or anyone wearing them.
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Air[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Fleshwort
articles.content: A short plant with gray stalks and olive-colored hairy leaves, the fleshwort thrives in areas where corpses lay. Often the plant will be seen on the site of battlefields, or where the dead were quickly buried.
articles.useCommon: Some healers use the fibrous body of the fleshwort to speed up the healing process during a short rest (a character may add 1d6 to the total amount of hit points recovered).
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Shadow[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Galvanized Tin
articles.content: A protective metal known for its resistance to rusting and corrosion. It's used in the creation of weather-resistant structures and machinery.

articles.title: Ghost Tendrils
articles.content:
A pale collection of tendrils, the fungus crawls along the ground looking for recent corpses. Once it locates a suitable host, it quickly spreads tendrils throughout the limbs of the creature, and begins a process of limited reanimation. 
The tendrils avoid sunlight whenever possible, as it causes the fungus to quickly dry out and perish. Thus, the fungus is often found in subterranean locations, such as crypts or in some locations of the Underdark. Local subterranean races that are aware of the fungus often dismember or burn their dead to prevent the fungus from spreading.
articles.useCommon: While within the corpse, the fungus moves about in whatever form of locomotion the corpse was capable of in life, although lack of fine motor control often causes the corpse to stumble about on all four limbs. The fungus then searches for other possible hosts to embed with spores, often attacking warm living targets. [p]Once embedded, the fungus require 1d6+4 hours to reach maturity where it can reanimate the host corpse.
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Life[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Goblin-Trap
articles.content: Often found in clusters, these plants resemble brown leathery vases sitting on the ground with ragged leaves splayed out around the base where thick roots grasp the earth. The tops are open, and if one should peer inside the glint of their golden seeds can be seen in the interior. However, these plants are traps, and many an explorer have come across the remains of smaller humanoids, their hands still clutching the golden seed pods.
articles.useCommon: If someone is unwise enough to reach into the pod, they must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC 15) or suffer 1d4 damage from the internal spines of the plant, and are then grappled. [p]10 points of damage must be inflicted upon the plant to free a trapped arm, the leathery surface providing a natural AC of 11. [p]Ripping the plant out of the ground requires a successful Strength (Athletics) check (DC 20) and inflicts another 1d4 damage upon the trapped victim.
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Shadow[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Goblinberry Bush
articles.content: Growing in high, desolate areas, the small bushes have dark green leaves that are nearly black, with silver undersides. The bushes sporadically sprout reddish-purple berries that have a strong sharp smell that often causes people to gag.
articles.useCommon: Mixing the juice of the berries with a significant quantity of water and honey makes an interesting drink that is served sporadically in Evereska and Baldur’s Gate. The juice can also be used as an astringent, if the patient can handle the smelL
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Fire[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Golden Lungwort
articles.content: The golden lungwort has large oval leaves, covered in small hairs and small pale blotches. It grows to a height of about 1 foot, and has small bell-shaped flowers with five petals which change color from pink to mauve to blue. Flowers of different colors are often found on the same plant.
articles.useCommon: This herb heals the user of all aches and pains at the cost of -1 to all Intelligence­based ability checks for the next 4 hours.
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Fire[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Gravefist
articles.content: A pale white mushroom with five knobby protrusions, the mushroom often resembles a skeletal hand reaching upward. When the mushroom grows in clusters, it appears as if numerous corpses are attempting to reach out of the soil The fungus predominantly grows in the Underdark, however it has also been seen in dark basements and unused root cellars. Several tales of superstition follow the fungus, with many believing it's appearance in your cellar heralds a coming death in the family.
articles.useCommon: There may be some truth to the 'coming death' belief as the mushrooms release toxic spores that slowly inflict 4d8 poison damage spread over a period of 2d8 days to creatures that sleep or live within 100 feet of this fungus.
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Earth[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Haven Steel
articles.content: A high-quality, versatile steel alloy that's the backbone of Haven's metalworks industry. Known for its exceptional strength and durability.

articles.title: Ironwood Coal
articles.content: Sourced from the @[Ironwood Forests](location:0bf0d950-e305-431e-8a21-b4a82bba7848), this coal burns hotter and longer than regular coal. Essential for the intense heat needed in Haven Ironworks.

articles.title: Ironworks Bronze
articles.content: A durable and hardy alloy of copper and tin. Bronze from Havens Ironworks is particularly prized for its acoustic properties and is often used in the construction of musical instruments.

articles.title: Kanishta
articles.content: A short plant that grows in tendrils across the ground sprouting broad fluffy leaves, the kanishta thrives in the cold environment. Growing beneath the ground is a thick, dark colored root.
articles.useCommon: Although it is extremely bitter tasting, chewing on the root provides temporary relief from the cold weather. [p]Those that chew it express a feeling of warmth passing through their body, and a sense of renewed strength, gaining resistance to cold and frost damage, and +1 to Strength checks for 4 hours.
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Earth[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Kingsfruit
articles.content:
The bark of this tree is a smooth rich brown, while the soft leaves are oval-shaped and sprout in small clusters. 

During the summer months, small violet flowers grow from the tree. During the autumn months, the tree produces a dark brown fruit with a tough exterior. 

The fruit has an extremely hard rind, requiring a blow from a hammer to open. Striking either end of the fruit causes the exterior to divide cleanly into six equal parts.
articles.useCommon: The inside of the fruit is golden-yellow, and quite sweet. Eating the fruit restores hit points equal to two of your hit dice and restores any hit dice expended from short rests.
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Water[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Knitbone
articles.content: A tall, stalky plant with purple flowers and long, hard, slightly sharp leaves.
articles.useCommon: The leaves and flowers can be ground into a poultice that, when applied to an injured creature, will recover 1 Hit Die and heal 1d10 hit points.
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Earth[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Lithium Grease
articles.content: Produced by @[alchemists](profession:758e627f-aaca-4874-80a5-7a28af7a97dd), lithium grease is a soft, greasy, grey compound. It is formed through a process that include lithium ore, animal fat and mineral oil.
articles.useCommon: Used by @[herbalists](profession:1848d3a7-6f83-44af-a930-7fd4c9e351d0) as a base for making [i]rare[/i] potions.

articles.title: Lusiri Blossom Kiss of Discord
articles.content: A herb with dull red leaves that resemble lips.
articles.useCommon: Ingestion causes 1d4 hours of hallucinations and the feeling that you must now go on a spirit quest. You feel pulled toward a far off destination.
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Water[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Obaddis Leaf
articles.content: A rare variety of holly able to retain magic
articles.useCommon: Can be used as Druidic focus. [p]As a component for spells that target plants, it doubles both area and duration.
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Life[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Oil of Vitriol
articles.content: Oil of Vitriol is a highly corrosive, slightly viscous, clear liquid produced by @[alchemists](profession:758e627f-aaca-4874-80a5-7a28af7a97dd) via a process of  roasting green vitriol in an iron retort.
articles.history: The study of vitriol began during ancient times when a list of types of vitriol classified them according to the substances' color. Some of the earliest discussions of the origin and properties of vitriol included its use in medicine and metallurgy
articles.useCommon: Diluted forms are used by @[herbalists](profession:1848d3a7-6f83-44af-a930-7fd4c9e351d0) as a base for making [i]uncommon[/i] potions.
articles.properties: Concentrated forms are extremely corrosive and will damage skin.
articles.taste: Unknown - anywon who has tried had their mouth severely burned
articles.odor: Odourless in dilute form, acrid in more concentrated form

articles.title: Pure Alcohol
articles.content: By taking distilled alcohol and applying mechanical and chemical filters, an @[alchemist](profession:758e627f-aaca-4874-80a5-7a28af7a97dd) can make alcohol in its purist form.
articles.useCommon: Dangerous to drink in significant quantity but still consumed by people with a death wish.  Used by @[herbalists](profession:1848d3a7-6f83-44af-a930-7fd4c9e351d0) as a base for making [i]very rare[/i] potions.
articles.properties: Highly flammable in liquid form and the vapor can be explosive.

articles.title: Quicksilver Lichen
articles.content: A slimy, glittering silver lichen.
articles.useCommon: Can be ground into a soluble, flavorless powder that can be added to food or drink to grant damage weakness to poison for 2d6 hours.
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Water[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Rattlestalks
articles.content: These tall stalks that grow out of stagnant water end in a thick bulb with a hard shell In the warmer months, the stalk withers, and the bulb falls off, to float around until the bulb softens and falls away to disperse the seeds within. The seeds sit freely in the shell and when shaken make a slight rattling noise, which is unnerving when the wind rushes through a cluster of rattlestalks.
articles.useCommon: Tribes that live in the swamps will often use the unnerving aspect of the rattlestalks to create fear inducing noisemakers that are then added to spears and other weapons.[p]When rattled, those that have no knowledge of the rattlestalks must make a DC 8 Wisdom saving throw, or be Frightened for 1d4 minutes.
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Air[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Rose Campion
articles.content: A green-brown herb.
articles.useCommon: Consuming this herb will protect against scorpion and spider stings for a period of three days, granting advantage on Constitution saving throws against poison from arachnids.
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Shadow[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Salamander Orchid
articles.content: This rare orchid is constantly on fire, and propagated by a talented spellcasters.
articles.useCommon: A creature proficient with the herbalism kit can apply the sticky pollen of the flower to a bladed weapon on a successful Intelligence (Nature) check (DC 15), causing it to deal an additional 1d6 fire damage for 8 hours. [p]Handling the salamander orchid without proper tools found in the herbalism kit causes 1d6 points of fire damage per minute.
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Fire[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Sand Vine
articles.content: A relatively rare rope-like seaweed found along temperate or warmer coasts, it grows both above and below water and is commonly rooted to a small rock.
articles.useCommon:
Can be dried and used as rope. [p]Using the herbalism kit, its juices can be extracted to produce vine oil, a topical anesthetic granting 5 temporary hit points for 1 hour. 
- Has the side effect that within 24 hours of use, a creature cannot stabilize on its own (so even with successful death saves, the creature or person must be assisted). 
- Regular use gives a creature a signature odor of salt and slightly-rotted fish.
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Shadow[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Sandberry Bush
articles.content: Small bushes with waxy green leaves, the sandberry bush grows in the remote reaches of the Shaar. It has small yellow berries that have a bitter taste, but can be brewed to make a soothing tea. The few nomadic tribes that pass through the Shaar pick the berries as a remedy for insomnia.
articles.useCommon: Acts as a sleep aid, if the consumer is willing, inducing a 6-hour sleep cycle with no negative side effects.
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Fire[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Seregon
articles.content: Blood-red flowers that bloom directly from their roots in rocky soil
articles.useCommon: Ingesting the flower’s nectar grants a creature 2d6 temporary hit points that last for 1 hour. This effect cannot be combined with any other effect that grants temporary hit points.
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Air[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Sunstone Creeper
articles.content: A vine that normally grows along the ground instead of climbing. The flower is distinct with its large, meaty yellow petals adorned with white stripes.
articles.useCommon: Ground cover
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Fire[/b]

articles.title: Tekkil
articles.content: A succulent swamp plant with fat red leaves
articles.useCommon: When chewed, the leaves have an analgesic effect with a sense of euphoria. You gain a +5 bonus to saving throws against fear, and are immune to the frightened condition for the next hour. [p]The numbing effect confers a -5 penalty to all Dexterity-based saving throws during this time.
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Life[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Tempered Copper
articles.content: This copper has been carefully treated in the Haven Ironworks furnaces to increase its resilience and conductivity. It's frequently used in the manufacture of various clockwork devices.

articles.title: Weirwood Bark
articles.content: A rare oak-like tree with leaves a silver sheen of brown on top and velvet black on the underside, viewed as sacred by dryads and treants. A weirwood can grow to heights in excess of two hundred feet, and the oldest living specimens are estimated to be more than six centuries old. The wood does not burn from non-magical fire, and imparts a warm clear tone to instruments made from it.
articles.useCommon:
It can replace oak or holly in any spell, and living weirwood has quadruple fire resistance (no means to preserve this quality after harvesting is known). 
Weirwood trees within range of a magical light source emits light as a candle for 1 minute after leaving the area.
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Earth[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making

articles.title: Wispstalks
articles.content: This rare fungus has become something of a fable among herbalists. It is reported to have a large, translucent blue bulbous cap growing atop a thin stem, and normally forms in small clusters deep within damp cave environments and forests.
articles.useCommon:
When consumed, the creature must make a Constitution saving throw (DC 13). 
- On a failed save, the creature takes 1d6 poison damage and nothing else happens except the creature coughing up what part of the fungus they tried to consume. 
- On a success the creature becomes transparent (but not invisible) for 1 hour, gaining a +10 bonus to Dexterity (Stealth) checks.
articles.properties: Can be processed to produce [b]Essence of Air[/b]
articles.manufacturing: Can be used in potion making
